Gene_Accession_Number.reason: Data availability explicitly gives AcPT1 accession as LC425153.

Source_Species.reason: AcPT1 was isolated from Artemisia capillaris.

Source_Species_Latin_Name.reason: The source species is explicitly Artemisia capillaris.

Applied_Species.reason: AcPT1 was analyzed in A. capillaris, expressed in Nicotiana benthamiana, and engineered into Saccharomyces cerevisiae.

Applied_Species_Latin_Name.reason: All three applied species names appear in the paper.

Omics_Data.reason: The gene was identified from RNA-seq, and Data availability gives DRA007286 for the raw RNA-seq reads.

Enzyme_Name.reason: The paper describes AcPT1 as a phenylpropane-specific PT and an artepillin C synthase.

Protein_Family_or_Domain.reason: AcPT1 is explicitly described as a UbiA superfamily membrane-bound PT with predicted N-terminal transit peptide and multiple transmembrane regions; Discussion mentions two Asp-rich motifs.

Biosynthetic_Pathway.reason: AcPT1 functions in prenylated phenylpropanoid/artepillin C biosynthesis.

Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway.reason: The Results explicitly place it in p-coumaric acid prenylation yielding drupanin and artepillin C.

Catalyzed_Reaction_Description.reason: The Results demonstrate stepwise transfer of two dimethylallyl groups from DMAPP to p-coumaric acid via drupanin to artepillin C, and direct conversion of drupanin to artepillin C.

Primary_Substrate.reason: p-Coumaric acid, drupanin, and DMAPP were all directly tested as substrates in vitro.

Primary_Product.reason: Drupanin and artepillin C were identified as products by HPLC and MS comparisons with standards.

Terminal_Metabolite.reason: Artepillin C is the final product emphasized throughout the paper.

Terminal_Metabolite_Class.reason: Artepillin C is a prenylated phenylpropanoid/simple phenylpropane derivative.

Terminal_Metabolite_Function.reason: The introduction explicitly lists reported bioactivities of artepillin C including antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, antioxidative, immunomodulatory, anti-carcinogenesis, anti-obesity, and anti-diabetes-related effects.

Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site.reason: Figure 2 shows artepillin C in leaves, stems, and flowers of A. capillaris; yeast experiments show p-coumaric acid and much drupanin in medium, while artepillin C is mostly in cells.

End_Product_Connection_Type.reason: AcPT1 directly catalyzes formation of the target product artepillin C.

Metabolic_Step_Position.reason: AcPT1 performs the terminal prenylation steps from p-coumaric acid to drupanin to artepillin C.

Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step.reason: The paper supports AcPT1 as a key terminal determinant and notes limitations in yeast expression/substrate accessibility, but does not explicitly designate it as a formal rate-limiting step.

Core_Phenotypic_Effect.reason: Results show AcPT1 enables biosynthesis of drupanin and artepillin C from p-coumaric acid, including production in engineered yeast.

Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species.reason: qRT-PCR showed expression in leaves, stems, and flowers with no clear statistically significant differences, though stems tended to be higher.

Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species.reason: The paper documents native expression in A. capillaris and recombinant expression in N. benthamiana and yeast.

Subcellular_Localization.reason: Microscopy supports plastid localization in plant cells; enzymatic assays used microsomes from heterologous expression systems, consistent with membrane association.

Applied_Promoters.reason: 35S promoter was used for transient expression in N. benthamiana via pGWB vectors, and GAL1/GAL10 promoters were used in yeast.

Environment_or_Treatment_Factor.reason: Galactose induction, exogenous 1.0 mM p-coumaric acid feeding, and Mg2+ in vitro are all explicitly described experimental conditions affecting activity/yield.

Core_Validation_Method.reason: All listed methods are directly described in Results/Methods: RNA-seq screening, RT-PCR cloning, qRT-PCR, GFP localization, transient expression, microsome assays, HPLC/LC-MS identification, substrate specificity, kinetics, and yeast engineering.

Other_Important_Info.reason: The paper supports all listed points: first identified plant phenylpropane-specific PT, single-polypeptide sequential di-prenylation, DMAPP preference over GPP/FPP, acceptance of ferulic acid, improved yeast production by TP removal, little effect of codon optimization, and drupanin release as intermediate.

Potential_Tradeoffs.reason: The yeast system showed poor intracellular p-coumaric acid availability and export of drupanin, limiting artepillin C productivity; no plant growth penalty is reported.

Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu.reason: The study explicitly positions AcPT1 as useful for microbial production/engineering of artepillin C, and shows TP truncation plus strengthened DMAPP supply improve output.

Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene.reason: All four summary points are directly supported by the Results and Data availability.
